fullText: arc AGI is the only benchmark out there that has not been completely saturated by artificial intelligence and they just launched ARC AGI 3 their new interactive AGI benchmark Humans can solve it at 100% AI less than 1% It is the coolest benchmark out there in my opinion and I'm going to tell you everything about it So I've talked about the ARC AGI benchmark quite a bit and now they're on their third iteration Let me just show you what the first two were Now the entire purpose of the ARC AGI benchmark is to test for AGI but more specifically to test for generalization which is right there in the acronym AGI artificial general intelligence That means AI that has the ability to take a little bit of learning and generalize it to multiple applications And if that doesn't make sense I'm going to explain all of this So this is ARC AGI 1 Basically what it is is you get a couple examples as a human or AI and you kind of look for the pattern and then apply that to another example So check this out So for this we can see three pink squares times two Then we can see for each of these batches of three squares to complete the puzzle you have to add a yellow one to the missing section to make a square Okay that seems pretty easy But let's see the second example and make sure that our intuition is correct Well look at that We have three of them this time And to complete the 2x two square we add a yellow single square to it And now all three are 2 x two squares So what does the third one look like well it seems pretty obvious especially for humans Looking at it how do we complete it we would add a yellow square here here here and here And that would be the solution Now if that seems really easy it's supposed to be for humans AI on the other hand struggles with these types of benchmarks Now for Arc AGI 2 they made it much harder Same kind of pattern same kind of approach but much more difficult And this benchmark has yet to be saturated So check this out You can see here is an example Here's the solution Here's the next example What would the solution be here's the key This is what I assume is a key It really doesn't tell you anything So for yellow there are no squares missing So I would say this gets yellow This gets yellow And yeah we can see that right here Next for the green we have a single empty square in the middle So looking none of them have a single empty square So how does that work well maybe it just means that the empty squares in the middle of the shape are connected So there we go One and two These last ones you can see there are two gaps in the middle Thus it's blue And then three for red And so we can kind of figure out what it would be down here So this just becomes much more difficult Now for the ARC AGI one leaderboard you can see it has been almost nearly saturated The best of the best models are coming out right here at about 93 94% solved Now again humans can do this at 100% So this isn't even saturated but it's very close Now on the x-axis we're seeing cost per task On the y-axis we're seeing scoring percentage Now let's look at ARC AGI 2 Here we go The top model GPT 5.4 Pro Extra High scores a 72% at a cost per task of $39 Very expensive And again humans can solve this very easily 100% And so we can see here's Gemini 3.1 Pro at 69% Here's Claude Opus 4.6 medium at 68% So still they're doing very very well but not near 100% This segment is sponsored by Microsoft I've started over a dozen companies and I sold my last one and so it's so weird to look back and see how I ran companies before artificial intelligence and now after artificial intelligence And really everything has changed from reviewing legal documents to automating different workflows the code that I write how I do research how I ideulate All of that before AI was much slower much more manual and took so much longer All of it is so different now that I am using AI every single day I save a huge amount of time which frees up my team for things like taste creativity and fun instead of grunt work With Microsoft 365 C-pilot business it really has just changed the entire way I do business and allowed me to scale up so much more quickly as a small team operating like a big one I can use Copilot for things like contract review It's integrated with Word and Excel so I could easily have it write documents or run financial analysis for me And I'll actually show you how to use it with my own prompts and outputs And I made a YouTube short about this going into more detail about how I'm using AI today So I'm going to drop a link to that in the description below So if you're an entrepreneur I really recommend checking out this video because the way that I work is so vastly different now And if AI seems intimidating that's okay It is absolutely worth learning because the payoff in time saved and quality of your work will be massive Now back to the video Now before I show you Arc AGI 3 which is completely different very unique very interesting I want to talk about a few things that make ARC AGI special as a benchmark One it really focuses on cost per task It's not enough just to spend all of your tokens You really want to be efficient with your learning That's really important And as I mentioned earlier it is very easy for humans to solve these problems but very difficult for artificial intelligence that is very unique as compared to all the other benchmarks If you look at some of the coding benchmarks the math benchmarks the science benchmarks the humans that get the best scores on those are the smartest people in the world the best coders in the world the best scientists the best mathematicians and then AI goes and beats them So it's the best versus the best However the average human can solve the ARC AGI benchmarks but AI can't It can't even get close Oh and by the way they're giving away $2 million if you can saturate the benchmark So there is certainly incentive to do so And that brings us to ARC AGI 3 the first interactive version of the ARC AGI benchmark They went from looking at examples and being able to generalize to another example to a fully interactive benchmark And I've actually played with it So here's what it looks like You get dropped in a video game essentially You get zero information about what to do in the video game You get a limited amount of turns to try to solve or play beat whatever you want to call it the video game You know what instead of me talking more let me just show you Hopefully I can get this right So let's play start We can use these arrows right here It seems we get reset help and select And again I get no information about what's happening The AI gets no information about what's happening But let's just look around before we even do anything So we have three dots over here We have this yellow bar Maybe it's a health meter Maybe it's a turn meter We have something in the bottom left that looks like maybe it can either be a mini map but more likely it's looking like it's matching this So maybe this is the destination of where I need to get to We have what looks to be like a little maze This is probably our character The plus Maybe it's this one right here Maybe this is our character And it seems like we need to get up there Let's try to move I'm just going to hit up arrow once Okay So we already learned a bunch of information One I hit up This guy moved This is now obviously our character I also noticed that this little bar dropped down which leads me to believe that might be a move meter So let's see Now I think maybe I just have to get up to here So let's continue Yeah So I moved again The yellow bar went down And let's just go all the way to the top That did not work So it flashed Something flashed here And obviously I'm not done because it didn't tell me I'm done Now one thing I notice is this is in a different orientation than this So maybe as part of the game I have to hit this plus to try to move the orientation of this before I actually get to it So let's reset and let's play I'm going to go this way now I'm going to try to hit that plus Okay there we go It hit the plus Now it looks like the goal is oriented the same Now let's move the rest of the way All right there we go And so that's it So you can see I had a lot of intuition I used a lot of logical deduction to try to figure out what to do And I only had a limited amount of turns to do that in but I was able to do it What did it take me 3 minutes to figure it out Not even And I was actually explaining it as we go So probably would have taken me about a minute to solve But how does AI do on it not well They actually tested it against all the Frontier models and they basically failed across the board GPT 5.4 0% Gemini 3.1 Pro preview 0% Grock 4.2 Claude Opus 4.6% 0% Human 100% Total actions 546 Let's look at what it looks like for GPT 5.4 to play it There it goes Okay so it took the first step like I did and it keeps trying to go back there and it's just not getting it It doesn't think to go to the plus which is just wild to me Why is it not thinking to do that it seems so obvious It seems so intuitive but that's because I have obviously decades of video game playing experience And so there we go It could not do it And you can see right here the top model is scoring a.3% That is GPT 5.4 four high coming in at over $5,000 to get that score Absolutely wild So they've released a paper They have a $2 million prize for the winner And if you want to try it out yourself you definitely can And by the way here are all the different games that you can play None of them are the same You get no information You just drop in and try to
